name: MJ-12 Documents
description: The MJ-12 (Majestic 12) documents are a collection of purported top-secret papers that first surfaced in 1984, claiming to reveal the existence of a highly classified group of scientists, military officials, and government leaders formed in 1947 by President Harry S. Truman to investigate unidentified flying objects (UFOs) and extraterrestrial life. The documents detail alleged crash retrievals, including the Roswell incident, and ongoing interactions with alien civilizations. Proponents argue the documents represent undeniable proof of a government cover-up regarding UFOs. However, mainstream scholars and investigators widely regard the MJ-12 documents as elaborate forgeries. Common criticisms include inconsistencies in formatting, signatures that do not match known examples, anachronistic language and terminology, and the lack of any corroborating evidence from declassified government archives. The documents' provenance is also highly suspect, tracing back to UFO researchers rather than official channels. While the MJ-12 mythos has significantly influenced UFO lore and popular culture, it remains firmly in the realm of conspiracy theory due to the overwhelming evidence of their fraudulent nature. The documents fueled speculation about hidden government agendas and secrecy surrounding UFO phenomena, contributing to a broader narrative of mistrust towards official narratives and the belief in a powerful, clandestine organization controlling information about extraterrestrial life. Despite repeated debunking efforts, the MJ-12 documents continue to be cited by some UFO enthusiasts as evidence of an ongoing conspiracy.
subtitle: Alleged secret UFO government group.

claims.claim: MJ-12 was formed in 1947 to investigate UFOs.
claims.criticism: No corroborating evidence exists outside the documents, and the documents themselves are considered forgeries.

claims.claim: The U.S. government recovered a crashed alien spacecraft near Roswell, New Mexico in 1947.
claims.evidence: Alleged references in the MJ-12 documents and witness testimonies.
claims.criticism: The Roswell incident is explained by the US military as the crash of a Project Mogul balloon. Witness accounts are often unreliable and contradictory.

claims.claim: The government is actively engaged in a cover-up of UFO and extraterrestrial phenomena.
claims.evidence: The existence of MJ-12 and the alleged suppression of information.
claims.criticism: The 'evidence' relies solely on the MJ-12 documents, which are widely discredited. The government has declassified thousands of UFO-related documents.

keyFigures.name: Stanton Friedman
keyFigures.role: Ufologist who initially promoted the authenticity of the documents.

keyFigures.name: Philip J. Klass
keyFigures.role: Debunker and skeptic who exposed inconsistencies and argued the documents were forgeries.

timeline.event: Alleged formation of MJ-12 after the Roswell incident.
The first MJ-12 document surfaces, sent anonymously to UFO researchers.
Continued debate and investigation into the authenticity of the documents.
